Command Example

!qradar-get-custom-properties field_name="AVT-App-Name" like_name="rule" limit=2
Context Example

{
"QRadar": {
"Properties": [

outputs.name: There is no context output for this command.
Retrieves a list of event regex properties.

outputs.description: The flag to indicate if the event regex property is generated by custom properties discovery engine. QRadar.Properties.creation_date Date
outputs.name: QRadar.Properties.auto_discovered

outputs.description: QRadar.Properties.datetime_format The date/time pattern that the event regex property matches. QRadar.Properties.description
outputs.name: The date when the event regex property was created.

outputs.description: The description of the event regex property. QRadar.Properties.id The sequence ID of the event regex property.
outputs.name: String

outputs.description: The ID of the event regex property. QRadar.Properties.locale The language tag of the locale that the Property matches.
outputs.name: QRadar.Properties.identifier

outputs.description: Date The date when the event regex property was last modified. QRadar.Properties.name
outputs.name: QRadar.Properties.modification_date

outputs.description: The name of the event regex property. QRadar.Properties.property_type The property type (STRING, NUMERIC, IP, PORT, TIME) of event regex property.
outputs.name: String

outputs.description: The flag to indicate if the event regex property is parsed when the event is received. QRadar.Properties.username The owner of the event regex property.
outputs.name: QRadar.Properties.use_for_rule_engine
